title: Projektcontroller Infrastrukturprojekte (m/w/d)
required_skills: Completed degree in business administration, engineering, or comparable qualification
Professional experience in project controlling, project management, or project steering of complex infrastructure/energy/large-scale projects
Sound project management knowledge and experience in scheduling and project control
Proficient use of MS Project and standard MS Office applications
Analytical and structured working style with strong communication and organisational skills
High willingness to travel with regular on-site presence in Lüneburg and Stockelsdorf
bonus_skills: Change management, quality management, and reporting experience within project organisations
matched_skills: Completed degree (Master of Management & Entrepreneurship, Leuphana) — satisfies education requirement
Project management knowledge: sprint planning and delivery governance at Hapag-Lloyd; 6-workstream programme coordination at HSBC — satisfies general PM knowledge requirement
Analytical and structured working style: KPI dashboards, budget variance tracking, executive reporting at HSBC — satisfies analytical/organisational skills requirement
Good English skills (C1) — satisfies English requirement
MS Office proficiency (Excel, PowerPoint) — satisfies MS Office requirement
Risk escalation and management at HSBC — partial match for project controlling responsibilities
Workshop/meeting facilitation mentioned at INP (local presence in Luneburg) — geographic advantage
Change management and internal communications: town hall events, sprint ceremonies at Hapag-Lloyd — partially satisfies bonus skills
missing_skills: No MS Project experience — explicitly required for scheduling and project control
No infrastructure or energy sector project experience
No Projektcontrolling (project controlling) role experience specifically
German B1 falls short of required fluent German (fliessende Deutschkenntnisse)
High travel requirement including regular on-site presence may be a practical constraint
No experience with large-scale infrastructure project documentation (Arbeitspakete, Terminplaene)
core_responsibilities: Analyse and control project progress; identify deviations and support countermeasure development
Prepare management reports, project analyses, and decision templates for project leadership and steering bodies
Plan, structure, and track work packages; maintain project schedules in MS Project
Support change, quality, and reporting management within the project organisation
Prepare and moderate workshops, coordination meetings, and project-related discussions
Collaborate closely with project management, planning, construction coordination, and other project participants
recommended_emphasis: Highlight the HSBC programme coordination — managing 6 workstreams, risk escalation, and prioritisation decisions — as closest evidence of project controlling competency
Explicitly mention Lüneburg as the candidate's base location (CV confirms this) — strong practical alignment with on-site requirement
Address the MS Project gap directly in a cover letter by noting willingness to upskill; highlight existing project tracking experience with JIRA
Strengthen the German language presentation — B1 is borderline; the candidate should demonstrate written German competency actively
summary: Weak match at 44.4%. The candidate's PMO coordination and governance experience at HSBC and Hapag-Lloyd transfers reasonably to the project controlling function, and Lüneburg residency is a genuine advantage for the on-site requirement. However, the German language gap (B1 vs fluent required), absence of MS Project skills, and lack of infrastructure/energy sector experience are material weaknesses. Borderline application — worth attempting given geographic proximity, but the German language gap needs mitigation.
